title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
How ULIP Prepares Single Parents Against Financial Uncertainties?
Being a single parent can be challenging. You have to give your child the love of a mother and a father. You have to guide your child, make it a responsible individual, and also give the important lessons of life so that your child grows up to be self-sufficient and knowledgeable.
Besides the moral and emotional duty, you have a financial duty towards your child too. Being a single parent, you are solely responsible for providing your child with the ideal resources when growing up. Moreover, you have to plan for a secured financial future for your child, which would not be disturbed if you were not around. Securing your child’s future in your absence is all the more important for single parents. While different investment plans help you build up a corpus for your child, a unit-linked life insurance policy can give you the extra edge.
What is a ULIP?
A ULIP is a life insurance policy that aims to create an investment corpus for your financial goals, in this case, a corpus for your child. These plans invest your premium in the capital markets so that you can earn inflation-adjusted returns in tandem with market performance. You get the flexibility of managing your investment as you see fit and even get liquidity through partial withdrawals.
Why are ULIPs beneficial for single parents?
For single parents, ULIPs can be the perfect addition to their financial portfolio, especially when planning their child’s future. Here are some reasons why –
Insurance + investment = ULIP
The ULIP is a unique investment plan that combines the benefits of investment returns with insurance protection security. The ULIP plan allows you to invest in the market and assures financial assistance to your loved ones if you are not around. As such, you get a dual benefit from the policy that other investment avenues fail to offer.
Secured corpus through child ULIPs
If your child's financial security is your sole concern, a child ULIP can be your best bet. The plan is designed to create a secured corpus for a child. To that intent, it usually comes with an inbuilt premium waiver benefit. This benefit waives off the future premium if the parent dies during the policy tenure. However, the plan does not stop. It continues till the chosen tenure, and the insurance company contributes the premium on the parent’s behalf. This ensures that the fund value keeps growing till maturity. On maturity, the available fund value is paid.
The plan, thus, ensures an undisturbed corpus creation even if the parent dies prematurely. As a single parent, the child ULIP plan can allow you to secure your child’s future.
Wealth creation through market-linked returns
ULIPs have different types of investment funds. Each of these funds invests in capital market securities that include equity, debt, money market instruments, etc. Moreover, the funds have a diversified portfolio and are professionally managed. You can use a ULIP calculator to estimate the returns from the same.
Thus, when you invest in ULIPs, you get to invest in a diversified portfolio of market-linked securities. These securities have the potential to deliver attractive returns as the market grows. You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the expected corpus at conservative interest rates and plan for your child’s needs. This return can help you build up an inflation-adjusted portfolio for your child’s future so that the cost of higher education, marriage, starting a career, etc., can be easily handled.
Flexibility of investment
With ULIPs, you get a host of flexible benefits to manage your money and investment. If you need funds for the small financial needs of your child, you can make partial withdrawals after the first five years of the plan are complete.
Secondly, based on the changing market dynamics, you can switch between the available funds to maximise your returns while minimising the associated risks and losses.
There’s also the option of additional investment through top-up premiums and the settlement option that allows you to redeem your maturity benefit in instalments for extended returns.
So, with ULIPs, you can manage your investments at your discretion and even draw on them when needed.
Tax benefits⁴:
Lastly, the tax benefits of a ULIP serve as the ultimate cherry on the cake. The premium paid for the policy is allowed as a deduction from the taxable income. The limit is 10% of the sum assured, subject to a maximum of Rs.1.5 lakhs under Section 80C1. This helps you reduce your taxable income and, consequently, your tax liability.
Secondly, switching your investment from one fund to another is also completely tax-free. You don’t pay any tax on the amount switched.
Thirdly, partial withdrawals do not attract tax2. Thus, you can make tax-free withdrawals for your financial needs.
Fourth, the death benefit received from a ULIP is completely tax-free, with no terms and conditions attached.
Fifth, the maturity benefit you get is tax-free, provided that the aggregate premium paid for all the ULIPs bought on or after 1st February 2021 does not exceed Rs.2.5 lakhs.
These tax benefits can create a tax-efficient corpus, help you save taxes, and increase your disposable income when investing.
Conclusion
The love for a parent cannot be undermined, even when the parent is single. You love your child and want to give them the better life. However, everything costs money, and while you can toil hard to earn, you need to secure your earnings too. The ULIP plan helps you do just that. It not only allows you to create a tax-efficient, inflation-adjusted, and market-linked corpus but also guarantees financial assistance after your demise. A child ULIP can be the right plan for securing your child’s future in your absence.
So, invest in the right ULIP and gift your child a secured future.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs.The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
How ULIP Prepares Single Parents Against Financial Uncertainties?
Being a single parent can be challenging. You have to give your child the love of a mother and a father. You have to guide your child, make it a responsible individual, and also give the important lessons of life so that your child grows up to be self-sufficient and knowledgeable.
Besides the moral and emotional responsibility, you have a financial responsibility towards your child too. Being a single parent, you are responsible for providing your child with the suitable financial resources while the child is growing up. Moreover, you may also have to plan for a secured financial future for your child, which may not be disturbed if you are not around. Securing your child’s financial future in your absence is all the more important for single parents. While different investment plans may help you build up a corpus for your child, a unit-linked life insurance policy may give you the extra edge.
What is a ULIP?
A ULIP is a life insurance policy that aims to create a market linked investment corpus for your financial goals, in the present case, a corpus for your child. These plans invest your premium in different market-linked funds so that you can earn returns subject to market performance. You get the flexibility of managing your market linked investment as you see fit and even get liquidity through partial withdrawals subject to policy terms & conditions.
Why are ULIPs may be considered beneficial for single parents?
For single parents, ULIPs may be the preferred addition to their financial portfolio, especially while planning their child’s financial future. Here are some reasons why –
1. Insurance + investment = ULIP
The ULIP is a unique insurance cum investment plan that combines the benefits of market linked investment returns with insurance protection security. The ULIP plan allows you to invest in the market-linked funds and assures financial assistance to your loved ones when you are not around. 2. Secured financial corpus through based Child Plans
○ If your child's financial security is your sole concern, a ULIP based child plan may be your bet. The plan is designed to create a secure corpus for a child’s financial future. Commonly, depending from insurer to insurer, it comes with an option of Waiver of Premium benefit2 which waives off future premiums in case of death of a parent. Thus, the policy continues till the chosen tenure and the fund value keeps growing till maturity, as per market conditions. On maturity, the available fund value is paid. ○ The plan, thus, ensures an undisturbed corpus creation even if the parent dies prematurely. As a single parent, the child ULIP plan may allow you to secure your child’s financial future. 3. Wealth creation through market-linked returns
○ ULIPs have different types of investment funds. Each of these funds invests in securities that include equity, debt, money market instruments, etc. Moreover, the funds have a diversified portfolio and are professionally managed. You can use a ULIP calculator to estimate the returns from the same.
○ Thus, when you invest in ULIPs, you get to invest in a diversified portfolio of market- linked securities. These securities have the potential to deliver returns as the market grows but with risk component associated with it. You can use the ULIP calculator to estimate the expected corpus at conservative interest rates and plan for your child’s financial needs. 4. Flexibility of investment
○ With ULIPs, you get a host of flexible benefits to manage your money and investment. If you need funds for the small financial needs of your child, you may make partial withdrawals after the first five years of the plan are complete subject to plan’s terms & conditions. ○ Secondly, based on the changing market dynamics, you can switch between the available funds to optimize your market linked returns while minimising the associated risks and losses. This is subject to policy terms & conditions. ○ There’s also the option of additional investment through top-up premiums by paying additional premium and the settlement option that allows you to redeem your maturity benefit in instalments for extended returns, subject to policy terms & conditions. ○ So, with ULIPs, you can manage your investments and even draw on them when needed, subject to terms & conditions of the policy 5. Tax benefits
○ Lastly, the tax benefits of a ULIP serve as the ultimate cherry on the cake. The premium paid for the policy is allowed as a deduction from the taxable income. The limit is 10% of the sum assured, subject to a maximum deduction of Rs.1.5 lakhs under Section 80C. This helps you reduce your taxable income and, consequently, your tax liability1. ○ Secondly, switching your investment from one fund to another is also completely tax-free. You don’t pay any tax on the amount switched. The tax implications would be triggered only at the time of receipt upon withdrawal/maturity or surrender in accordance with section 45(1B) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. ○ Thirdly , the death benefit received from a ULIP is tax-free, . ○ Fourth, the maturity benefit/partial withdrawal benefit you get is tax-free, provided that the aggregate premium paid for all the ULIPs bought on or after 1st February 2021 does not exceed Rs.2.5 lakhs and policy is satisfying conditions mentioned under Section 10(10D) of Income Tax Act, 1961. ○ These tax benefits can create a tax-efficient corpus, help you save taxes, and increase your disposable income when investing.
Conclusion
The love for a parent cannot be undermined. You love your child and want to give them the better life. However, that may cost money, and while you can toil hard to earn, you may need to secure your earnings too. The ULIP plan may help you do that. It not only allows you to create a tax- efficient, and market-linked corpus but also helps in ensuring financial assistance after your demise. A ULIP based child plan can be the preferred plan for securing your child’s financial future in your absence.
So, consider investing in the suitable ULIP and gift your child a secured financial future.
Disclaimer: In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
